NCT00036439.name: ACT-1
NCT00036439.group: Infliximab 5 mg/kg IV (0, 2, 6 wk) | TNFi (moderate-severe UC; vs Placebo; ACT-1 wk 8 clinical remission)

allOutcomes.title: Clinical remission (total Mayo <=2 with no subscore >1) at wk 8 infliximab 5 mg/kg vs placebo (ACT-1 primary; OR)

NCT00036439.snippet: Rutgeerts P et al. NEJM 2005;353:2462-2476 (ACT-1 + ACT-2). First biologic in UC. Remission 38.8% vs 14.9% at wk 8.

CRITERIA: "Inclusion Criteria:~* Diagnosis of UC for at least 3 months prior to baseline.~* Confirmed diagnosis of moderately or severely active UC, as assessed by the modified Mayo score (MMS).~* Demonstrated an inadequate response to, a loss of response to, or an intolerance to conventional or to biologic therapy for UC.~* If female, must meet the contraception requirements.~Exclusion Criteria:~* Participants with a current diagnosis of Crohn's disease or inflammatory bowel disease-unclassified (indeter...

NMA_CONFIG.outcome_label: Clinical remission at induction timepoint (week 6–12, trial-specific) — OR scale
NMA_CONFIG.note: k=9 Pbo-anchored induction trials. Pbo-star network. Primary clinical remission definition varies across trials (Mayo-score total vs modified Mayo vs adapted Mayo) — declared as moderate transitivity concern. Timepoint varies wk 6 (PURSUIT-SC, GEMINI-1) – wk 12 (LUCENT-1).

title: RapidMeta Gastroenterology | UC Biologics/Advanced-Therapy Induction-Remission NMA v1.0
